source_documents: https://www.rbi.org.in (Master Directions on Frauds - Classification & Reporting, incl. Early Warning Signals)
category: Occupational fraud - abuse of approval authority / control override
risk_signals: Employees approving their own transactions or overriding system controls
Use of dormant, suspense, or customer accounts to route unauthorised entries
Transactions structured just under the employee's approval limit
analyst_reasoning: Insiders with approval authority can bypass the very controls meant to check them, self-approving or overriding to move funds. The signature is control override and self-dealing - segregation-of-duties failures - often hidden in dormant or suspense accounts and sized to stay under personal limits.
investigation_steps: Review self-approvals, control overrides, and segregation-of-duties exceptions in logs
Monitor dormant, suspense, and internal accounts for unexplained movement
Detect transactions clustered just below the approver's authority limit
decision: Revoke excess entitlements, enforce maker-checker separation, and pursue disciplinary/legal action on confirmed abuse.
outcome: Authority abuse persists until segregation-of-duties and override monitoring catch it; entitlement governance is central.
lessons_learned: Self-approval and control override are the defining insider-authority-abuse signals
Dormant and suspense accounts are favoured hiding places and must be monitored
Segregation of duties and least-privilege entitlements are the core preventive controls
